No hypotension, no infection signals. Vascular surgery consult and heparin initiation suggest thrombotic rather than infectious etiology.

37-year-old female with bradycardia (HR 56-66) and stable vitals. No fever, tachycardia, hypotension, or organ dysfunction. Pattern inconsistent with sepsis—more suggestive of vagal tone, beta-blockade, or athletic baseline.

Severe discordance detected: vitals appear stable (HR 83, RR 15, SpO2 96%) but Glasgow Coma Score shows catastrophic decline from 15 to 4-6 over same timeframe. Combined with mild leukocytosis (WBC 11.71) and elevated creatinine (1.69), pattern highly suspicious for septic encephalopathy or CNS infection requiring immediate evaluation.
